name: Prepare DFG grant proposal
description: Objective: Develop a comprehensive and competitive grant proposal for the Deutsche Forschungsgemeinschaft (DFG). Scope: Include all necessary sections, such as project description, methodology, budget, and team qualifications. Steps: 1. Review DFG guidelines. 2. Draft proposal sections. 3. Obtain internal reviews. 4. Finalize and submit. Deliverables: Complete DFG grant proposal.

name: Secure University of Bonn internal funding
description: Objective: Obtain internal research funding from the University of Bonn. Scope: Prepare application materials, present project to relevant committees, and secure funding approval. Steps: 1. Identify internal funding opportunities. 2. Prepare application. 3. Present to committee. 4. Secure approval. Deliverables: Approved internal funding application.

name: Develop budget justification
description: Objective: Create a detailed and justified budget for the research unit. Scope: Include all anticipated expenses, such as personnel, equipment, supplies, and travel. Steps: 1. Estimate costs for each category. 2. Prepare budget justification document. 3. Obtain internal review. 4. Finalize budget. Deliverables: Complete budget justification.

name: Explore alternative funding sources
description: Objective: Identify and explore alternative funding sources in case of delays or insufficient funding from primary sources. Scope: Research potential funding opportunities from foundations, industry partners, and other government agencies. Steps: 1. Research funding opportunities. 2. Assess eligibility. 3. Prepare preliminary applications. Deliverables: List of potential alternative funding sources.

name: Prepare ethics application documents
description: Gather all necessary documents, including the study protocol, consent forms, and data management plan, for submission to the Ethics Committee.

name: Submit ethics application to committee
description: Formally submit the prepared ethics application to the Ethics Committee of the University of Bonn.

name: Address committee feedback and revisions
description: Respond to any feedback or requests for revisions from the Ethics Committee and resubmit the application as needed.

name: Obtain final ethics approval
description: Secure the final approval from the Ethics Committee, ensuring all requirements are met and documented.

name: Identify Potential Recruitment Channels
description:
Objective: Identify a diverse range of potential recruitment channels for the parasomnia study.
Scope: This includes online platforms, partnerships with medical professionals, and community outreach programs.
Steps: Research potential channels, assess their suitability, and prioritize based on reach and cost-effectiveness.
Deliverables: A list of prioritized recruitment channels with contact information and estimated reach.

name: Assess Channel Suitability and Feasibility
description:
Objective: Evaluate the suitability and feasibility of each identified recruitment channel.
Scope: This includes assessing the target audience, cost-effectiveness, and potential ethical considerations.
Steps: Conduct market research, analyze historical data, and consult with experts.
Deliverables: A report summarizing the suitability and feasibility of each channel, including recommendations for implementation.

name: Establish Formal Agreements
description:
Objective: Formalize agreements with selected recruitment channels.
Scope: This includes negotiating terms, drafting contracts, and obtaining necessary approvals.
Steps: Contact channel representatives, negotiate terms, and finalize agreements.
Deliverables: Signed agreements with selected recruitment channels.

name: Develop Recruitment Materials
description:
Objective: Create compelling recruitment materials tailored to each channel.
Scope: This includes designing advertisements, writing brochures, and developing online content.
Steps: Develop materials, obtain feedback, and revise as needed.
Deliverables: A set of recruitment materials for each selected channel.

name: Review Literature on Stringency Criteria
description:
Objective: Conduct a comprehensive review of existing literature to identify relevant and validated stringency criteria for NREM parasomnia research.
Scope: This includes searching databases (PubMed, Scopus, Web of Science) for studies on parasomnia, sleep disorders, and related neurological conditions. The review will focus on inclusion/exclusion criteria used in previous studies, their rationale, and their impact on sample characteristics and event capture.
Steps:
1. Define search terms and keywords.
2. Conduct literature search.
3. Screen articles for relevance.
4. Extract data on stringency criteria.
5. Summarize findings and identify potential criteria for the current study.
Deliverables: A summary report of the literature review, including a list of potential stringency criteria and their rationale.

name: Consult Sleep Disorder Neurologist
description:
Objective: Obtain expert opinion from a neurologist specializing in sleep disorders to validate the proposed stringency criteria and ensure their clinical relevance.
Scope: This involves scheduling a consultation with a neurologist, presenting the proposed criteria, and discussing their potential impact on participant selection and data interpretation.
Steps:
1. Identify a suitable neurologist.
2. Schedule a consultation.
3. Prepare a presentation of the proposed criteria.
4. Conduct the consultation.
5. Document the neurologist's feedback and recommendations.
Deliverables: A written summary of the neurologist's feedback and recommendations, including any suggested modifications to the stringency criteria.

name: Pilot Test Stringency Criteria
description:
Objective: Conduct a pilot test of the proposed stringency criteria to assess their feasibility and impact on participant recruitment and event capture.
Scope: This involves applying the criteria to a small sample of potential participants and evaluating the number of eligible participants, the characteristics of the recruited sample, and the number of parasomnia events captured.
Steps:
1. Recruit a small sample of potential participants.
2. Apply the stringency criteria.
3. Assess the number of eligible participants.
4. Collect data on participant characteristics and event capture.
5. Analyze the data and evaluate the impact of the criteria.
Deliverables: A report summarizing the results of the pilot test, including the number of eligible participants, the characteristics of the recruited sample, and the number of parasomnia events captured.

name: Refine Criteria Based on Pilot Data
description:
Objective: Refine the stringency criteria based on the results of the pilot test and the neurologist's feedback.
Scope: This involves analyzing the pilot test data, considering the neurologist's recommendations, and making any necessary adjustments to the criteria to optimize participant recruitment and event capture.
Steps:
1. Analyze the pilot test data.
2. Review the neurologist's feedback.
3. Identify potential modifications to the criteria.
4. Discuss the proposed modifications with the research team.
5. Finalize the refined stringency criteria.
Deliverables: A final version of the stringency criteria, incorporating the results of the pilot test and the neurologist's feedback.

name: Define EEG montage and sensor placement
description: Determine the optimal EEG montage and sensor placement strategy to maximize signal quality and minimize artifacts. This includes selecting appropriate electrode locations, considering head size and shape variations, and establishing standardized procedures for sensor application.

name: Establish PSG data acquisition parameters
description: Define the parameters for polysomnography (PSG) data acquisition, including sampling rates, filter settings, and impedance thresholds. This involves optimizing the settings for each channel (EEG, EOG, EMG, ECG, respiratory sensors, etc.) to ensure accurate and reliable data collection.

name: Develop data quality control procedures
description: Establish procedures for monitoring and ensuring the quality of acquired data. This includes implementing real-time artifact detection, developing protocols for addressing data quality issues, and establishing criteria for data rejection or re-acquisition.

name: Pilot test data acquisition protocols
description: Conduct pilot studies to evaluate the effectiveness and feasibility of the data acquisition protocols. This involves collecting data from a small number of participants, analyzing the data quality, and refining the protocols based on the results.

name: Document data acquisition procedures
description: Create detailed documentation of the data acquisition procedures, including step-by-step instructions, troubleshooting guides, and quality control checklists. This documentation will serve as a reference for research staff and ensure consistency in data collection across participants and time points.

name: Analyze workload based on participant projections
description:
Objective: Determine the required staffing levels based on the projected number of participants and the data collection activities involved.
Scope: This includes analyzing the time required for each data collection session, data annotation, and participant management.
Steps: 1. Estimate the time required for each data collection activity. 2. Project the number of participants enrolled per month. 3. Calculate the total workload in hours per month. 4. Determine the number of staff required to meet the workload demands.
Deliverables: A detailed workload analysis report with staffing recommendations.

name: Develop flexible staffing model
description:
Objective: Create a staffing model that can be adjusted based on actual recruitment rates and data volume.
Scope: This includes defining different staffing scenarios and identifying triggers for adjusting staffing levels.
Steps: 1. Define different staffing scenarios based on recruitment rates. 2. Identify triggers for adjusting staffing levels (e.g., number of participants enrolled, data volume). 3. Develop a plan for onboarding and offboarding staff as needed.
Deliverables: A flexible staffing model document with defined scenarios and adjustment triggers.

name: Consult sleep research staffing experts
description:
Objective: Obtain expert advice on optimal staffing levels and best practices for managing research staff.
Scope: This includes identifying and engaging with external consultants who have experience in sleep research staffing.
Steps: 1. Identify potential consultants. 2. Contact consultants and schedule consultations. 3. Obtain recommendations on staffing levels and best practices.
Deliverables: A consultant report with recommendations on staffing levels and best practices.

name: Document staffing coverage model
description:
Objective: Create a comprehensive document outlining the staffing coverage model, including roles, responsibilities, and coverage schedules.
Scope: This includes defining the roles and responsibilities of each staff member, creating coverage schedules, and documenting procedures for handling staff absences.
Steps: 1. Define the roles and responsibilities of each staff member. 2. Create coverage schedules for each role. 3. Document procedures for handling staff absences and emergencies.
Deliverables: A staffing coverage model document with roles, responsibilities, and coverage schedules.

name: Identify potential risks
description:
Objective: Identify all potential risks that could impact the project.
Scope: Consider technical, financial, operational, environmental, and security risks.
Steps: Conduct brainstorming sessions with stakeholders, review project plans and documentation, and consult with experts.
Deliverables: A comprehensive list of potential risks.

name: Assess risk probability and impact
description:
Objective: Evaluate the likelihood and potential impact of each identified risk.
Scope: Quantify the probability and impact using a risk assessment matrix.
Steps: Assign probability and impact scores to each risk, prioritize risks based on their overall risk score.
Deliverables: A prioritized list of risks with probability and impact assessments.

name: Develop mitigation strategies
description:
Objective: Create strategies to mitigate or eliminate the identified risks.
Scope: Develop specific actions to reduce the probability or impact of each risk.
Steps: Brainstorm mitigation options, evaluate the feasibility and cost-effectiveness of each option, and select the most appropriate strategies.
Deliverables: A detailed risk mitigation plan with specific actions, responsibilities, and timelines.

name: Document risk mitigation protocol
description:
Objective: Create a formal document outlining the risk mitigation protocol.
Scope: Include risk identification, assessment, mitigation strategies, and monitoring procedures.
Steps: Compile all relevant information into a comprehensive document, review and approve the document with stakeholders.
Deliverables: A finalized risk mitigation protocol document.

name: Implement and monitor risk mitigation
description:
Objective: Put the risk mitigation strategies into action and track their effectiveness.
Scope: Execute the actions outlined in the risk mitigation plan, monitor risk levels, and adjust strategies as needed.
Steps: Assign responsibilities for implementing mitigation actions, track progress against timelines, and report on risk levels.
Deliverables: Regular reports on risk mitigation progress and effectiveness.

name: Define data annotation categories
description: Establish a clear and comprehensive set of categories for annotating parasomnia events, including specific criteria for each category. This will ensure consistency and accuracy in the annotation process.

name: Develop annotation guidelines document
description: Create a detailed document outlining the annotation categories, criteria, and procedures. This document will serve as a reference for annotators and ensure consistency in their work.

name: Pilot test annotation workflow
description: Conduct a pilot test of the annotation workflow with a small subset of data to identify any issues or areas for improvement. This will help to refine the workflow and ensure its effectiveness.

name: Refine guidelines based on pilot test
description: Based on the results of the pilot test, revise the annotation guidelines and workflow to address any identified issues or areas for improvement. This will ensure that the final workflow is as effective and efficient as possible.

name: Train annotators on workflow
description: Provide comprehensive training to annotators on the established workflow and guidelines. This will ensure that they are fully equipped to perform their tasks accurately and consistently.

name: Conduct thorough property inspection
description:
Objective: Identify potential structural issues before purchase.
Scope: Engage a qualified inspector to assess the property's condition.
Steps: Schedule inspection, review report, address concerns.
Deliverables: Inspection report, list of necessary repairs.

name: Engage licensed renovation contractor
description:
Objective: Secure a contractor with experience in similar renovations.
Scope: Identify, interview, and select a qualified contractor.
Steps: Research contractors, request bids, review proposals, sign contract.
Deliverables: Signed contract, renovation plan.

name: Secure preliminary building permit approvals
description:
Objective: Obtain necessary permits for renovation work.
Scope: Prepare and submit permit applications to relevant authorities.
Steps: Gather documentation, submit application, address queries, obtain approval.
Deliverables: Approved building permits.

name: Establish relationships with multiple suppliers
description:
Objective: Mitigate supply chain risks for construction materials.
Scope: Identify and establish relationships with multiple suppliers.
Steps: Research suppliers, request quotes, negotiate terms, establish accounts.
Deliverables: Supplier list, price agreements.

name: Design Equipment Layout
description: Develop a detailed layout plan for the equipment within the renovated property, ensuring optimal workflow and safety. This includes specifying the placement of PSG, EEG, video monitoring, and other sensors. Deliverable: Approved equipment layout diagram.

name: Prepare Site for Equipment Installation
description: Ensure the renovated property is ready for equipment installation. This includes verifying electrical wiring, network connectivity, and environmental controls (temperature, humidity, lighting). Deliverable: Site readiness checklist.

name: Calibrate and Test Equipment
description: Calibrate all equipment according to manufacturer specifications and conduct thorough testing to ensure proper functionality. This includes PSG, EEG, video monitoring, and environmental sensors. Deliverable: Calibration and testing reports.

name: Integrate Equipment with IT Infrastructure
description: Connect all equipment to the local NAS and data analysis software. Configure data acquisition and storage protocols. Deliverable: Integrated equipment and IT system.

name: Identify Equipment Vendors
description:
Objective: Identify potential vendors for each piece of equipment required for the research unit.
Scope: Research and compile a list of at least three vendors for each equipment type (PSG, EEG, sensors, etc.).
Steps: Online research, vendor directories, conference contacts.
Deliverables: Vendor list with contact information and preliminary pricing.

name: Obtain Equipment Quotes
description:
Objective: Obtain detailed quotes from selected vendors for all required equipment.
Scope: Contact vendors, request quotes, and clarify specifications.
Steps: Prepare RFQs, send to vendors, track responses.
Deliverables: Detailed quotes from each vendor, including pricing, delivery timelines, and warranty information.

name: Evaluate Vendor Proposals
description:
Objective: Evaluate vendor proposals based on price, quality, and delivery timelines.
Scope: Compare quotes, assess vendor reputation, and negotiate terms.
Steps: Create a scoring matrix, review proposals, conduct vendor interviews.
Deliverables: Vendor selection report with justification for each choice.

name: Place Equipment Orders
description:
Objective: Place orders with selected vendors and track delivery.
Scope: Prepare purchase orders, submit to vendors, and monitor shipment status.
Steps: Generate POs, send to vendors, track shipments.
Deliverables: Confirmed purchase orders and delivery schedules.

name: Verify Equipment Specifications
description:
Objective: Verify that delivered equipment matches specifications and is in good working order.
Scope: Inspect equipment upon arrival, conduct initial testing, and document any discrepancies.
Steps: Unpack equipment, compare to PO, perform basic functionality tests.
Deliverables: Equipment verification report with any issues noted.

name: Unpack and Inventory Equipment
description:
Objective: Unpack all procured equipment and verify its completeness against the purchase orders.
Scope: This includes all PSG, EEG, video, and sensor equipment.
Steps: 1. Unpack each item. 2. Check against packing list. 3. Document any discrepancies.
Deliverables: Inventory list with confirmation of receipt and condition.

name: Calibrate and Test Equipment
description:
Objective: Calibrate all equipment to ensure accurate data collection.
Scope: This includes PSG, EEG, video, and sensor equipment.
Steps: 1. Follow calibration procedures for each device. 2. Test equipment using phantoms or simulated data. 3. Document calibration results.
Deliverables: Calibration reports for each piece of equipment.

name: Configure Equipment Settings
description:
Objective: Configure equipment settings according to the study protocol.
Scope: This includes PSG, EEG, video, and sensor equipment.
Steps: 1. Define optimal settings for each device. 2. Configure software and hardware. 3. Document settings.
Deliverables: Configuration files and documentation.

name: Integrate Equipment with IT Infrastructure
description:
Objective: Integrate all equipment with the IT infrastructure for data storage and analysis.
Scope: This includes network connectivity, data transfer protocols, and data storage configuration.
Steps: 1. Connect equipment to the network. 2. Configure data transfer protocols. 3. Test data transfer. 4. Configure data storage on NAS.
Deliverables: Functional data transfer and storage system.

name: Design Network Architecture
description: Define the network topology, addressing scheme, and security protocols for the research unit's IT infrastructure. This includes selecting appropriate network devices (routers, switches, firewalls) and configuring network services (DNS, DHCP).

name: Configure Servers and Storage
description: Set up and configure the local NAS with 50TB storage capacity, including RAID configuration, user access controls, and data backup procedures. Install and configure necessary server software (e.g., database server, web server).

name: Implement Data Security Measures
description: Implement data encryption, access controls, and intrusion detection systems to protect sensitive participant data. Ensure compliance with GDPR and other relevant data privacy regulations. Conduct regular security audits and vulnerability assessments.

name: Install and Configure Software
description: Install and configure data analysis software, video-EEG monitoring software, and other necessary applications. Ensure compatibility between different software components and provide training to research staff on software usage.

name: Test and Validate IT Infrastructure
description: Conduct thorough testing of the IT infrastructure to ensure network connectivity, server performance, data security, and software functionality. Validate that the infrastructure meets the research unit's requirements and is ready for data collection.

name: Draft Job Descriptions
description: Create detailed job descriptions for each research staff position, outlining responsibilities, qualifications, and required skills. This includes technicians, postdocs, and the study coordinator.

name: Post Job Openings
description: Advertise the job openings on relevant online platforms, professional networks, and university job boards to attract a diverse pool of qualified candidates.

name: Screen Applications
description: Review all applications received and shortlist candidates who meet the minimum qualifications and possess the desired skills and experience.

name: Conduct Interviews
description: Conduct interviews with shortlisted candidates to assess their suitability for the positions, evaluate their research experience, and determine their fit within the research team.

name: Onboard New Hires
description: Complete the onboarding process for selected candidates, including background checks, paperwork, and introductions to the research team and facilities.

name: Develop PSG scoring training module
description: Create a comprehensive training module on polysomnography (PSG) scoring, covering AASM guidelines and specific considerations for NREM parasomnias. This includes creating video examples, practice datasets, and quizzes to assess understanding.

name: Create EEG artifact recognition training
description: Develop a training program focused on identifying and mitigating EEG artifacts, including muscle artifact, eye movement artifact, and electrode artifact. This will involve creating visual aids, interactive exercises, and real-world examples.

name: Establish inter-rater reliability protocol
description: Define a standardized protocol for assessing and maintaining inter-rater reliability in event scoring. This includes establishing clear scoring criteria, implementing a dual-rater system, and conducting regular reliability checks.

name: Train staff on data privacy compliance
description: Conduct training on data privacy regulations, including GDPR, and implement data encryption and access controls. This will involve creating training materials, conducting workshops, and establishing clear data handling procedures.

name: Outline Training Modules
description: Define the scope and objectives of each training module, including key learning outcomes and assessment methods. This involves identifying the specific skills and knowledge required for research staff to effectively perform their duties related to parasomnia research.

name: Develop Module Content
description: Create detailed training materials for each module, including presentations, handouts, videos, and interactive exercises. Ensure the content is accurate, up-to-date, and aligned with the latest research findings and best practices in sleep research.

name: Design Hands-On Exercises
description: Develop practical exercises and simulations that allow research staff to apply their newly acquired knowledge and skills in a realistic setting. This may include mock data analysis scenarios, equipment operation simulations, and role-playing exercises for participant interaction.

name: Create Assessment Tools
description: Develop assessment tools to evaluate the effectiveness of the training program and identify areas for improvement. This may include quizzes, exams, performance evaluations, and feedback surveys.

name: Identify Potential Referral Sources
description:
Objective: Identify and list potential referral sources for participant recruitment.
Scope: Includes sleep clinics, neurology practices, general practitioners, and online communities.
Steps: Research local and regional healthcare providers, compile contact information, and assess their suitability as referral partners.
Deliverables: A comprehensive list of potential referral sources with contact details and preliminary assessment.

name: Develop Recruitment Materials
description:
Objective: Create compelling and informative recruitment materials.
Scope: Includes brochures, flyers, website content, and social media posts.
Steps: Design visually appealing materials, write clear and concise text, and ensure compliance with ethical guidelines.
Deliverables: A suite of recruitment materials ready for distribution.

name: Establish Agreements with Referral Sources
description:
Objective: Formalize partnerships with key referral sources.
Scope: Includes negotiating referral agreements, outlining responsibilities, and establishing communication protocols.
Steps: Contact potential referral sources, present the study, negotiate terms, and finalize agreements.
Deliverables: Signed referral agreements with key partners.

name: Create Online Recruitment Platform
description:
Objective: Develop a user-friendly online platform for recruitment.
Scope: Includes website, online surveys, and social media presence.
Steps: Design and build the platform, create engaging content, and ensure data security.
Deliverables: A fully functional online recruitment platform.

name: Develop recruitment materials
description:
Objective: Create compelling recruitment materials to attract potential participants.
Scope: Design flyers, brochures, website content, and social media posts.
Steps: 1. Draft content. 2. Design visuals. 3. Obtain ethics approval. 4. Print/publish materials.
Deliverables: Approved recruitment materials.

name: Engage with referral sources
description:
Objective: Establish relationships with neurologists and sleep clinics for participant referrals.
Scope: Contact potential referral sources and present the study.
Steps: 1. Identify potential referral sources. 2. Prepare presentation materials. 3. Schedule meetings. 4. Follow up with referral sources.
Deliverables: List of engaged referral sources.

name: Implement online advertising campaign
description:
Objective: Utilize online platforms to reach potential participants.
Scope: Design and launch targeted online advertisements.
Steps: 1. Identify target demographics. 2. Create ad content. 3. Launch campaign on relevant platforms. 4. Monitor ad performance.
Deliverables: Active online advertising campaign.

name: Conduct community outreach events
description:
Objective: Raise awareness about the study within the local community.
Scope: Organize and host community events to recruit participants.
Steps: 1. Identify suitable event locations. 2. Plan event activities. 3. Promote the event. 4. Host the event.
Deliverables: Completed community outreach events.

name: Screen potential participants
description:
Objective: Assess eligibility of potential participants based on inclusion/exclusion criteria.
Scope: Conduct initial screening interviews and administer questionnaires.
Steps: 1. Develop screening protocol. 2. Conduct phone screenings. 3. Schedule in-person assessments. 4. Review assessment results.
Deliverables: List of eligible participants.

name: Schedule Participant Monitoring Sessions
description: Coordinate with participants to schedule convenient and appropriate monitoring sessions, considering their availability and sleep patterns. This includes confirming appointments, providing pre-session instructions, and addressing any participant concerns.

name: Conduct Polysomnography (PSG) Recordings
description: Perform overnight polysomnography (PSG) recordings in the residential unit, ensuring proper electrode placement, equipment calibration, and continuous monitoring throughout the night. This includes troubleshooting any technical issues that may arise during the recording.

name: Collect Video-EEG and Sensor Data
description: Simultaneously collect video-EEG data, contact-free mattress sensor data, infrared camera recordings, ambient microphone recordings, and environmental sensor data during PSG sessions. Ensure proper synchronization of all data streams.

name: Store and Backup Collected Data
description: Securely store all collected data on the local NAS with 50TB storage capacity, implementing robust data backup procedures to prevent data loss. This includes regular data backups, data encryption, and access control measures.

name: Monitor Participant Well-being During Sessions
description: Continuously monitor participants for any signs of distress or adverse events during monitoring sessions. Implement safety protocols to address any medical emergencies or safety concerns that may arise.

name: Establish Emergency Response Protocol
description: Develop a detailed protocol for responding to medical emergencies or adverse events during sleep studies. This includes defining roles and responsibilities, establishing communication channels, and ensuring access to necessary medical equipment and personnel.

name: Implement Pre-Screening Procedures
description: Conduct thorough pre-screening of participants to identify potential health risks or contraindications for sleep studies. This includes reviewing medical history, conducting physical examinations, and administering questionnaires to assess psychological well-being.

name: Provide Participant Education
description: Provide detailed explanations of the study procedures, potential risks, and safety measures to participants before they enroll in the study. This includes obtaining informed consent and addressing any questions or concerns they may have.

name: Monitor Participants During Sleep Studies
description: Continuously monitor participants during sleep studies to detect any signs of distress or adverse events. This includes using video and audio monitoring equipment, as well as trained personnel to observe participants' behavior and physiological responses.

name: Address Participant Concerns Promptly
description: Establish a system for participants to report any concerns or discomfort they may experience during sleep studies. Ensure that these concerns are addressed promptly and effectively by trained personnel.

name: Establish Data Security Protocols
description: Objective: Implement robust data security measures to protect participant data. Scope: Includes encryption, access controls, and regular security audits. Steps: 1. Define security requirements. 2. Implement encryption. 3. Configure access controls. 4. Schedule regular audits. Deliverables: Secure data storage and access system.

name: Implement Data Backup and Recovery
description: Objective: Ensure data integrity and availability through regular backups and a recovery plan. Scope: Includes defining backup frequency, storage location, and recovery procedures. Steps: 1. Define backup schedule. 2. Select backup storage. 3. Implement backup procedures. 4. Test recovery process. Deliverables: Functional data backup and recovery system.

name: Ensure GDPR Compliance
description: Objective: Adhere to GDPR regulations for data privacy and protection. Scope: Includes obtaining consent, managing data access, and handling data breaches. Steps: 1. Obtain informed consent. 2. Implement data access controls. 3. Establish breach response plan. 4. Conduct regular compliance checks. Deliverables: GDPR-compliant data management system.

name: Monitor Data Quality and Integrity
description: Objective: Continuously monitor data for errors and inconsistencies. Scope: Includes implementing data validation procedures and regular audits. Steps: 1. Define data validation rules. 2. Implement data validation procedures. 3. Conduct regular data audits. 4. Correct data errors. Deliverables: High-quality and reliable participant data.

name: Develop Annotation Guidelines
description: Create detailed and consistent guidelines for annotating parasomnia events in EEG and video data. This includes defining event types, scoring criteria, and examples. The objective is to ensure consistency and accuracy across all annotators.

name: Train Annotators
description: Conduct training sessions for all annotators to familiarize them with the annotation guidelines and software. This includes hands-on practice with sample data and feedback sessions. The goal is to achieve a high level of inter-rater reliability.

name: Perform Data Annotation
description: Annotate the collected EEG and video data according to the established guidelines. This involves identifying and scoring parasomnia events, marking relevant features, and documenting any uncertainties. The deliverable is a fully annotated dataset.

name: Assess Inter-Rater Reliability
description: Evaluate the consistency of annotations across different annotators. This involves calculating inter-rater reliability scores and identifying areas of disagreement. The objective is to ensure the quality and validity of the annotated data.

name: Refine Annotation Workflow
description: Based on the inter-rater reliability assessment, refine the annotation guidelines and training procedures. This may involve clarifying ambiguous criteria, providing additional examples, or implementing new quality control measures. The goal is to improve the accuracy and consistency of future annotations.

name: Clean and Prepare Data
description:
Objective: Ensure data quality and consistency for analysis.
Scope: Handle missing values, outliers, and inconsistencies.
Steps: 1. Identify missing values and outliers. 2. Impute or remove missing data. 3. Correct inconsistencies in data formats. 4. Standardize data units.
Deliverables: Cleaned and prepared dataset.

name: Perform Descriptive Statistics
description:
Objective: Summarize key characteristics of the data.
Scope: Calculate descriptive statistics for relevant variables.
Steps: 1. Calculate mean, median, mode, standard deviation, and range for each variable. 2. Generate histograms and box plots to visualize data distributions. 3. Identify potential patterns and anomalies.
Deliverables: Descriptive statistics report and visualizations.

name: Conduct Statistical Modeling
description:
Objective: Identify relationships between variables and test hypotheses.
Scope: Apply appropriate statistical models to the data.
Steps: 1. Select appropriate statistical models (e.g., regression, ANOVA). 2. Fit models to the data. 3. Evaluate model performance and significance. 4. Interpret model results.
Deliverables: Statistical models and interpretation report.

name: Visualize Analysis Results
description:
Objective: Create visualizations to communicate findings effectively.
Scope: Generate charts, graphs, and tables to present analysis results.
Steps: 1. Select appropriate visualization techniques. 2. Create visualizations using statistical software or visualization tools. 3. Refine visualizations for clarity and impact. 4. Prepare presentation-ready figures.
Deliverables: Visualizations of analysis results.

name: Define Event Criteria
description: Establish clear, objective criteria for identifying and classifying parasomnia events to ensure consistency and accuracy in triage.

name: Select Algorithm
description: Evaluate and select appropriate machine learning algorithms for event detection and triage based on performance metrics and computational efficiency.

name: Train Model
description: Train the selected machine learning model using annotated data to accurately identify and classify parasomnia events.

name: Integrate with System
description: Integrate the trained event-triage tool with the existing data acquisition and analysis system for seamless operation.

name: Define Benchmark Metrics
description: Establish clear and relevant metrics for evaluating the performance of the event-triage tools. This includes sensitivity, specificity, precision, and F1-score. Define acceptable performance thresholds for each metric.

name: Prepare Gold Standard Dataset
description: Create a high-quality, manually annotated dataset of parasomnia events to serve as the ground truth for benchmarking. This involves selecting a representative sample of data and having it independently scored by multiple experts to ensure accuracy and reliability.

name: Run Benchmarking Experiments
description: Execute the event-triage tools on the gold standard dataset and record their performance according to the defined metrics. This involves configuring the tools, running them on the dataset, and collecting the results.

name: Analyze and Report Results
description: Analyze the benchmarking results to determine the strengths and weaknesses of the event-triage tools. Prepare a report summarizing the findings, including recommendations for improvement.

name: Outline manuscript structure and content
description: Develop a detailed outline including sections, subsections, key arguments, and supporting evidence for each publication. This ensures a logical flow and comprehensive coverage of the research findings.

name: Draft initial manuscript sections
description: Write the first draft of each section of the manuscript based on the outline. Focus on clarity, accuracy, and conciseness in presenting the research methods, results, and interpretations.

name: Review and revise manuscript drafts
description: Conduct thorough reviews of the drafted sections, focusing on content accuracy, clarity, and consistency. Incorporate feedback from co-authors and subject matter experts to improve the overall quality of the manuscript.

name: Finalize manuscript and prepare for submission
description: Address all remaining comments and suggestions, ensure adherence to journal guidelines, and format the manuscript for submission. Obtain final approval from all co-authors before submitting to the target journal.

name: Format manuscript for target journal
description: Adjust the manuscript's formatting (e.g., font, spacing, citation style) to comply with the specific guidelines of the chosen target journal.

name: Write cover letter
description: Compose a compelling cover letter highlighting the significance of the research and its suitability for the target journal.

name: Submit manuscript online
description: Upload the manuscript and all required supplementary materials through the journal's online submission system.

name: Track submission status
description: Monitor the progress of the manuscript through the peer review process and respond to any requests from the journal editor.

name: Identify relevant conferences and workshops
description:
Objective: Identify conferences and workshops suitable for presenting the research findings.
Scope: Search for conferences and workshops related to sleep research, neurology, and data analysis.
Steps:
1. Use online search engines and databases to identify potential conferences.
2. Review conference websites and calls for abstracts.
3. Evaluate conferences based on relevance, audience, and presentation opportunities.
Deliverables: A list of potential conferences and workshops.

name: Prepare and submit abstracts
description:
Objective: Prepare and submit abstracts to selected conferences and workshops.
Scope: Write concise and informative abstracts summarizing the research findings.
Steps:
1. Develop a template for abstracts.
2. Write abstracts based on the research findings.
3. Obtain feedback from co-authors.
4. Submit abstracts to selected conferences.
Deliverables: Submitted abstracts to conferences and workshops.

name: Develop presentation materials
description:
Objective: Develop presentation materials for accepted abstracts.
Scope: Create visually appealing and informative slides or posters.
Steps:
1. Create a presentation outline.
2. Develop slides or posters with key findings and visuals.
3. Practice the presentation.
Deliverables: Presentation slides or posters.

name: Present findings at conferences
description:
Objective: Present the research findings at conferences and workshops.
Scope: Deliver engaging and informative presentations.
Steps:
1. Travel to the conference location.
2. Deliver the presentation.
3. Answer questions from the audience.
Deliverables: Presentation delivered at the conference.

name: Summarize Key Findings
description: Compile a concise summary of the study's key findings, including statistical significance and practical implications. This will form the basis of the report's executive summary and main conclusions.

name: Draft Report Sections
description: Write individual sections of the final report, including introduction, methods, results, discussion, and conclusion. Each section should be well-structured and supported by relevant data and literature.

name: Integrate and Edit Report
description: Combine the drafted sections into a cohesive and well-edited final report. Ensure consistency in style, formatting, and referencing. Address any gaps or inconsistencies in the content.

name: Review and Approve Report
description: Circulate the completed report for review by the Principal Investigator and other key stakeholders. Incorporate feedback and obtain final approval before dissemination.
